speaker: Rob Moore
words: This side of the following tape was recorded on July 5th, 1973 at the home of Mr. Rudolph Nordby in Genesee, Idaho. The voices on the tape are those of Rudolph Nordby and the interviewer, Rob Moore.

speaker: Rob Moore
words: That story you told about being dropped off at the farm place in Palouse, pretty interesting to wonder if you could talk about that again.

speaker: Rudolph Nordby
words: When we moved in, when you came in to, when you came by train in T and what happened then?

speaker: Rudolph Nordby
words: We came in by train in Tuchaloos and then of course we hired a cab with a driver to take it out. This was in February and the road was just breaking up, breaking holes. We'd be going along there and we were supposed to be going along there. We'd be driving, we'd be mingling around in this place.

speaker: Rudolph Nordby
words: The interesting thing about that was that my grandmother was there and of course she taught nothing like Norwegian.

words: She scolded this fellow with his driving, so I said, that is Genesee's driving. It went on a lot. His name was Genesee.

words: No, he didn't speak Norwegian. She of course had no idea that Norwegian would ever have that here.

speaker: Rudolph Nordby
words: It was kind of interesting.

speaker: Rudolph Nordby
words: It was six and a half mile drive.

words: Did he ever say anything back to her? Did he ever tell her that he spoke Norwegian?

words: Yeah, he did. He talked Norwegian afterwards.

speaker: Rudolph Nordby
words: Then of course he'd done some terrible things.

Well, of course we had to get out and start buying boxes and equipment, which was very mediocre, of course. Most of it was used machine and things were done.
